response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo is primarily a web and full-stack developer whose career has been built on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. The mandatory C++ requirement is almost entirely unmet — the only relevant experience is a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 writing C/C++ components, with C++ absent from the skills section entirely. There is no Windows-platform development history, no SQL Server or Oracle depth, and no capital markets or fixed-income domain exposure; RIDE Capital involved investment tooling in Germany but bears no resemblance to the fixed-income derivatives or investment-banking systems described in the job.
strengths: Strong software-craftsmanship fundamentals (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) applied consistently across roles
Demonstrated ability to work in low-latency, high-throughput systems at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Broad multi-language background (Java, DLang, Node.js, Swift) shows adaptability to new stacks
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — approximately six months over a decade ago; the language does not appear in the candidate's own skills list
Zero capital markets, fixed income, or derivatives domain knowledge anywhere in the employment history
No evidence of Windows-platform development or SQL Server/Oracle usage, both of which are mandatory requirements

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain
No Windows or enterprise DB experience
